title: Vamorolone: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text:
es; itching; swelling of face, eyes, lips, or throat; difficulty swallowing or breathing confusion extreme changes in mood changes in personality inappropriate happiness depression changes in weight (gain or loss) stomach pain shortness of breath sudden weight gain swelling of feet, ankles, or lower legs changes in vision Vamorolone may slow growth and development in children. Your child's doctor will watch his or her growth carefully. Talk to your child's doctor about the risks of giving vamorolone to your child.
People who use vamorolone for a long time may develop glaucoma or cataracts. Talk to your doctor about the risks of using vamorolone and how often you should have your eyes examined during your treatment.
Vamorolone may increase your risk of developing osteoporosis. Talk to your doctor about the risks of using this medication.
Vamorolone may cause other side effects. Call your doctor if you have any unusual problems while taking this medication.
If you experience a serious side effect, you or your doctor may send a report to the Food and Drug Administration's (FDA) MedWatch Adverse Event Reporting program online ( https://www.fda.gov/Safety/MedWatch ) or by phone (1-800-332-1088).
What should I know about storage and disposal of this medication?
